creator: pauls@utdallas.edu
text:
--On Tuesday, August 12, 2008 09:23:02 -0400 Wesley Shields <wxs@FreeBSD.org> 
wrote:

> On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 11:02:08PM -0500, Paul Schmehl wrote:
>> --On August 12, 2008 12:45:44 PM +0900 Tomoyuki Sakurai
>> <cherry@trombik.org> wrote:
>>
>> > Sorry if my PR sounds offending.
>> >
>>
>> It doesn't.  Besides, I'm not easy to offend.  :-)
>>
>> > On Mon, Aug 11, 2008 at 09:32:22PM -0500, Paul Schmehl wrote:
>> >>
>> >> That is correct.  I was asked to fix the security/bro port because it
>> >> was  out of date.  Since doing that, I have discovered that bro is much
>> >> more  complex than my port allows for.  So, I began working on
>> >> security/bro-devel, but Chris Lee, who was working with me on some
>> >> patches, volunteered to take it over.  I don't know what, if anything,
>> >> he  has done with the port since.
>> >
>> > All I need is a working port; respecting user's choice, better current
>> > practice, common standard in ports tree. If his port is available, fine.
>> > The development branch (I'm not talking about svn tree) is "stable"
>> > since the last release, 2007, Jul. If he has made any progress, I'd
>> > really love to know.
>> >
>> >> The port requires someone who not only uses
>> >> bro and understands its intricacies but can also deal with source code
>> >> patching to get the software to work on FreeBSD.
>> >
>> > Additionally, some feedbacks to the upstream will make it better. The
>> > port shouldn't have many patches. The upstream is developing on FreeBSD.
>>
>> That's what I don't understand.  The port required a number of patches to
>> fix various problems.  I'm not skilled enough in C or C++ to do that, so
>> it really should be handled by someone with at least some programming
>> experience.
>
> My initial inquiry was to see who the maintainer should be since the
> submitter of the PR and the maintainer of the submission did not match.
> I'm not concerned right now with what the port needs; I'm more concerned
> with who I should be working with to make sure the port is well done.
> Obviously that person should also make sure the port meets users needs
> to the best of his/her (I'll help where I can) ability.
>
> So who is that person going to be?  After we figure that out I'll work
> with that person to get this port in the tree.
>
> -- WXS

I think either Tomoyuki or Chris.  Chris has offered to develop the bro-devel 
port, but I don't know what progress he's made with that, and I know he's very 
busy with other projects.

f Tomoyuki wants to take over security/bro and improve that port, I have 
absolutely no objection to that.  If he'd also like to create the 
security/bro-devel port, I'll let him and Chris work that out between them.

-- 
Paul Schmehl (pauls@utdallas.edu)
Senior Information Security Analyst
The University of Texas at Dallas
http://www.utdallas.edu/ir/security/
